message:
feat(anthropic): multi-auth-mode provisioning automation with self-check and doctor diagnostics

Implement the same provisioning automation pattern for the Anthropic
connector that was added for Microsoft 365 and Telegram (90c681b),
supporting dual authentication modes: direct API key and secretless
credential_id for egress proxy injection.

Anthropic client (client.rs):
- Introduce AnthropicAuth enum with ApiKey and CredentialId variants,
  centralizing auth header injection via apply_auth() instead of inline
  header calls in execute_with_retry() and execute_streaming()
- Add health_check() performing a minimal /v1/messages call (1 token,
  haiku model) to validate credentials without side effects
- Custom Debug impl that redacts API keys for safe diagnostics logging

Anthropic connector (connector.rs):
- Add AnthropicConfig struct with from_params() enforcing mutual
  exclusivity between api_key and credential_id
- Implement handle_doctor() with 6 named diagnostic checks:
  configuration, client_initialized, base_url, auth_mode,
  network_constraints (api.anthropic.com or localhost), and
  credential_injection status
- Implement handle_self_check() returning SelfCheckReport with
  degraded status for not-configured and credential_id modes, plus
  live health_check validation for API key mode
- Enrich handle_health() with auth and base_url fields
- Add ~270 lines of new tests covering configure (4 modes), doctor
  (4 scenarios), and self-check (5 scenarios including wiremock mocks)

Wire up "doctor" and "self_check" message dispatch in main.rs.
Apply rustfmt formatting cleanup to Telegram connector.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -99,6 +99,8 @@ async fn handle_message(connector: &mut AnthropicConnector, message: &str) -> se
         "configure" => connector.handle_configure(params).await,
         "handshake" => connector.handle_handshake(params).await,
         "health" => connector.handle_health().await,
+        "doctor" => connector.handle_doctor().await,
+        "self_check" => connector.handle_self_check().await,
         "introspect" => connector.handle_introspect().await,
         "invoke" => connector.handle_invoke(params).await,
         "simulate" => connector.handle_simulate(params).await,
